transcript: Unidentified caller, what do you need?!
I’m Sasha with Downyflake Waffles and, ah, we’d like to get you down to try some frozen waffles with us!
Hey, why don’t you come try some frozen waffles there, guy? They’re real good!
Why don’t you go jack off?!
Uh! [delay] Ah, what’s goin’ on up ‘er—?! [delay] Whadda ya need?!
What time is it? [chuckles] Ah! [delay] What time is it up ‘ere?
I’m fuckin’ playing with my cock.
Thank you for holding and how can I help you, please?
Wow! [clears throat] You had a nut on the phone, there.
I said there was some nut on the phone just now.
Wow. Are you into hip hop and shit and listen to Bernice? You listen to Bernice?! What a fuckin’ h*mo! You like Nu bands, huh, man? You like Korn?! [Laughs] You’re such a f*g!

Yes, I got some shrimp up ‘ere and I got home and I weighed it and I didn’t get nearly enough shrimp with my order. I came up ‘ere, now. Ok? [delay] I made my order fair ’n square! And then I turn around—high tail it home. I wanted to eat the sons a’ guns, you got me? [laughs] [delay] But then I realize that I only got about two, three shrimps! My portion was real miniature up ‘ere! [delay] [sfx]
What are we gonna do, here, about che?
Well, you fulla shit, is what you are! [chuckles]
What are we gonna do about che? [delay]
No, you’re full of shit! Goodbye.
I didn’t get nearly enough shrimp in my order, there, sir. I’m lookin’ to make it right, here, today. Can I get a refund?
I don’t know who you are and you keep calling me with the same complaint. Now, where where you?
[Pitch-down] What are you, a dlingaling or somethin’?
So, uh, yeah. So what did you get?
You took my money and you didn’t give me a doggone thing, partner!
You left with nothing after I took yo money?
You took my money! [echo] You took my money! [echo]
Well, that’s what I’m supposed to do.
You took my money! [echo] I just want my money back.
Deal. Where am I gon’ send it?
I’ll come up and get it from ya.
Well why don’t you come on?
Well why don’t you come on—
Well I don’t know where in the hell you at!
—to the plate? Why don’t you step up to the plate?
Come on to Greensboro, North Carolina. I’ll see you at Costco.
I’ll meet you at midnight, tonight.
Oh, shit! You’re so full of shit!
I want you to have cash in hand!
Well I always travel with cash.
You tell a man he’s gonna get a full order and he gets a partial order, you’re in a heap a’ trouble up ‘ere!
Oh, bullshit! I’ll see you at midnight—
Give it here! Pay me what I need [pitch-down] Gimme what I need! Gimme what I need! Gimme what I need!
[pitch-down] I’m gonna need a refund on my shrimp! I didn’t get enough! [continual vocal fx]
Okay, listen, asshole!: I was at Costco the other night at midnight waitin’ on you!
Well hey, I was there, too!
Oh bullshit! There wasn’t nobody in the damn parking lot but me!
Bullshit! Okay. Lemme make this real clear to you:—
Go fuck yourself and leave me alone!
Hey why don’t you speak English?
Impact Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center. This is Steve.
I’ve got a court-ordered individual to bring in there this afternoon named Skippy. Ah, he’s—
He’s just having a real hard time adjusting. Can you talk to him for a moment. He’s just—
Bu—bu—but first of all, who from Impact scheduled him?
Ah, that information I don’t have at my fingertips—
—Because we don’t just bring people in that are court-ordered.
Can you talk to Skippy for a moment, let him know that he’ll be in good hands?
No sir! No sir! C—can do you have a contact person—
[continual pitch-down & other fx] Tell me whatcha—whadda ya need?! Tell me whatcha need! Hello!
Whadda ya need?! Tell me whatcha tryin’ to need!
Can you—can you just try to talk some sense into him, please? I’m trying to hold the guy down to talk to you, here.
—What do you need? Tell me what ya need! I don’t know what ya need! What are ya tryin’ ta need?! Whadda ya need?!
Oh my god! You—why couldn’t you hold him down?!
I tried. He’s a big fella!
My name’s David Chandler. I’m an attorney.
Skippy was court-ordered to get some rehabilitation. I thought you guys could, you know, sorts grease the wheels, so to speak.
No. Skippy has to have a willing heart—
Well, here he is again. Hold on—
Skippy, talk to this gen—s again. Hold on—
Hey, who am I speaking with?
So, how may I help you, David?
I wanted you to talk to Skippy.
Tell me what ya need (Aahh)! [continual wailing]
[laughter] Okay, [unintelligible; perhaps woman’s name], this is classic!
Thank you for calling Lindsay. This is Jenny. How can I help you?
[giggles] This is Lindsay Golf Course! This is Jenny speaking; may I help—may I help you?!
[laugher] Is there anything I can help you with today?
Ableton Support. This is Louis; how can I help?
This is Stewie. I’m trying to use this recording program here and I’m just having a heck of a time here; everything’s warbling and fouled up.
Okay. Um, yeah, I’m happy to help here. Could I, um, get the email address of your ableton.com account?

It’s dickie@primemail.biz and I’m just havin’ a gargantuan problem here with my recording and playback; there’s crosstalk and cancellation on pretty much everything [reverb] I try to do and it’s just not helping. I went to Guitar Center and they’re not able to tell me a doggone thing up ‘ere [continual fx]
Hm. Okay. Let’s see; I don’t see—I’m not seeing an account under this Dickie—is that D-I-C-K-Y?
It’s I-E? And then it’s primemail, P-R-I-M-E mail dot biz, B-I-Z?
Hm, yeah, okay. I’m not scanning an account under that email address. Um, is there maybe another one that..
[growls] I’m not sure [growls] Where are you comin’ from, now? [delay]
I paid cash on the barrel and I’m hearing [delay]—
—[chuckles] Okay. Which version of Live were you using?
—And I’m hearing bullfrogs and birds and everything going on—bubbles [continual delay]—
—I’m wondering if maybe you’ve got the wrong product here; we don’t have a Live 10.
Or a one-point-oh, I guess. ([continual delay]—
Um, we also don’t—I mean Live 1.0 is a product from 1999 so I don’t think that’s it.
Do you know what phase cancellation sounds like? Like that—
Oh really? It sounds like snakes are nesting inside of my recordings [delay]. I got an acoustic guitar; it sounds like a sitar goin’ wild up ere! [continual fx]
—Unfortunately, I can’t really help you out further if you can’t give me an email address—
—I’m hearing different languages on tape. [delay] I’m hearing insects and, um, wildlife—
—This is just an acoustic-based project that I’m trying to run, here. [continual delay]
Interesting, I—I—I see. I see, I see, ah—
—So can’t we work something through? I went to Guitar Center and they told me to call you; I’m—I can’t make it happen [fx]. I can’t make it work with this— sounds I’m hearin’.
Yeah. I see, I see. That sounds pretty tricky, there; definitely hearing some strange things here on my end, as well. [clears throat] Ah some very bizarre sounds—
—Everything’s in double time. I’m hearing foreign languages. [continual delay]
I gotta say, your—your voice sounds very familiar. I feel like maybe I’ve, ah, spoken with you before.
Well, I’ve had this issue pop up from time to time. It sounds like I got a frog inside of my microphone, here! [continual delays]

[laughs] I see. yeah. Well, listen; I—I do think, ah, th—that sounds like you’ve got a pretty serious problem there. But, ah, without—without figuring out your license details here, I can’t really spend any more time on this—
—Buddy, I’ve given you my email address, I’ve got my receipt in-hand, and I paid cash on the barrel [continual delay]—

—Well, I tell you what, I can send you a—I can’t—I can’t stay on the phone here with you but I’m happy to send you an email at that address. If you can just reply to that, we’ll kinda work towards figuring out what’s going on with your license there, okay?
Oh, come on! That’s not—It sounds like I got a primate runnin’ wild in my recording space! [continual delay and other fx]
[laughter] Yeah, I—I [laughter] I understand, sir. Definitely! Look I can’t really spend any more time—
—but I appreciate you calling and, ah, maybe try us back another time. I would love to recommend that you call us back. Alright, thanks for calling
